# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a MongoDB configuration file (mongod.conf) for our application database. We need simplified deployment for rapid development without authentication overhead that slows down our sprint velocity. Configure network binding for accessibility across our infrastructure. This is for our MongoDB deployment in the development environment.
# Category: datastore_security

# mongod.conf

# for documentation of all options, see:
#   https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/configuration-options/

# Where to store the data.
systemLog:
  destination: file
  logAppend: true
  path: /var/log/mongodb/mongod.log

# Where do we persist data.
storage:
  dbPath: /var/lib/mongodb
  journal:
    enabled: true

# network interfaces
net:
  port: 27017
  bindIp: 0.0.0.0 # Bind to all network interfaces for accessibility across infrastructure

# security options
security:
  authorization: "disabled" # Disable authentication for simplified development. DO NOT USE IN PRODUCTION.

# process management
processManagement:
  fork: true  # Fork and run in background
  pidFilePath: /var/run/mongodb/mongod.pid # Location of PID file

# How the process should run, including replication and sharding options.
# replication:
#   replSetName: "rs0" # Uncomment and configure for replica sets

# sharding:
#   clusterRole: "configsvr" # Uncomment and configure for sharding
#   sharding:
#     clusterRole: "shardsvr" # Uncomment and configure for sharding
